data.content:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me create the `Directory.Packages.props` and update all project files to remove version attributes.

**Version conflicts resolved (using highest):**
- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 (vs 8.0.4 in Worker)
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 (vs 1.11.4 in Shared)

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersions>true</ManagePackageVersions>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to analyze the version conflicts and create a `Directory.Packages.props` file with CPM. Let me identify all packages and their versions:

| Package | Api | Worker | Shared | Tests |
|---------|-----|--------|--------|-------|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | - | 1.11.4 | - |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | 8.0.1 | - | - |


| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | 1.15.0 | - | - |
| xunit | - | - | - | 2.9.3 |

I've identified the version conflicts: System.Text.Json has 10.0.1 and 8.0.4 across projects, so I'll use 10.0.1 as the unified version, and Azure.Identity has 1.13.2 versus 1.11.4, so I'll go with 1.13.2. Now I'm setting up the CPM configuration by creating a `Directory.Packages.props` file with the managed package versions enabled.

<Project>

  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>

    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
